verses.text: The congregation journeys to Rephidim, where there is no water. The people chide Moses: give us water. He answers: why tempt the LORD? But they thirst, and murmur — wherefore brought you us out of Egypt, to kill us and our children with thirst? Guidance: Same pattern, new setting. The complaint at Marah, then over bread, now over water. The wilderness keeps surfacing whether trust will hold.

The congregation journeys to Rephidim, where there is no water. The people chide Moses: give us water. He answers: why tempt the LORD? But they thirst, and murmur — wherefore brought you us out of Egypt, to kill us and our children with thirst? Guidance: Same pattern, new setting. The complaint at Marah, then over bread, now over water. The wilderness keeps surfacing whether trust will hold.

The congregation journeys to Rephidim, where there is no water. The people chide Moses: give us water. He answers: why tempt the LORD? But they thirst, and murmur — wherefore brought you us out of Egypt, to kill us and our children with thirst? Guidance: Same pattern, new setting. The complaint at Marah, then over bread, now over water. The wilderness keeps surfacing whether trust will hold.

Moses cries to the LORD: what shall I do? They are almost ready to stone me. The LORD: go before the people, take elders, take the rod with which you struck the river. I will stand before you on the rock in Horeb; strike the rock, and water shall come. Guidance: The same rod that brought judgment now brings provision. God's instrument doesn't change — its work does. Trust the rod even when the assignment shifts.

I will stand before you on the rock in Horeb; strike the rock, and water shall come. Guidance: The same rod that brought judgment now brings provision. God's instrument doesn't change — its work does. Trust the rod even when the assignment shifts.

Moses cries to the LORD: what shall I do? They are almost ready to stone me. The LORD: go before the people, take elders, take the rod with which you struck the river. I will stand before you on the rock in Horeb; strike the rock, and water shall come. Guidance: The same rod that brought judgment now brings provision. God's instrument doesn't change — its work does. Trust the rod even when the assignment shifts.

He called the place Massah and Meribah — testing and contention — because of the chiding of the children of Israel, and because they tempted the LORD, saying: is the LORD among us, or not? Guidance: The deepest question of their thirst is not about water. It is whether God is here. Name your real question; don't disguise it as the surface complaint.

Then came Amalek and fought with Israel in Rephidim. Moses to Joshua: choose men, go out, fight with Amalek. Tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of God in my hand. Guidance: Joshua first appears here — already trusted with the battle while Moses holds the rod. Mentor and successor stand together: one fights, one intercedes.

Then came Amalek and fought with Israel in Rephidim. Moses to Joshua: choose men, go out, fight with Amalek. Tomorrow I will stand on the top of the hill with the rod of God in my hand. Guidance: Joshua first appears here — already trusted with the battle while Moses holds the rod. Mentor and successor stand together: one fights, one intercedes.

Joshua did as Moses said. Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up the hill. When Moses held up his hand, Israel prevailed; when he let it down, Amalek prevailed. They put a stone under him, and Aaron and Hur held his hands up — one on each side, steady until sundown. Guidance: Even the man of God needs his arms held up. Intercession is not solitary work. Find the two who will sit on either side and hold.

Joshua did as Moses said. Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up the hill. When Moses held up his hand, Israel prevailed; when he let it down, Amalek prevailed. They put a stone under him, and Aaron and Hur held his hands up — one on each side, steady until sundown. Guidance: Even the man of God needs his arms held up. Intercession is not solitary work. Find the two who will sit on either side and hold.

And Joshua discomfited Amalek and his people with the edge of the sword. Guidance: The battle below is won by the prayer above. Joshua's sword and Moses' steady arms are part of the same victory. God uses both.

The LORD to Moses: write this for a memorial in a book, and rehearse it in the ears of Joshua — I will utterly put out the remembrance of Amalek from under heaven. Guidance: Some battles get recorded so the next generation will know the enemy's name and pattern. Joshua will need this story; write it down.

Moses built an altar and called it Jehovah-nissi — the LORD my banner. He said: because the LORD has sworn, the LORD will have war with Amalek from generation to generation. Guidance: Victory becomes a name for God. The banner you fly over your hardest battle becomes a name you call God ever after. He is not a one-time deliverer; He is a banner for the long war.
